Paragraph.Sentence: A gardener plants rose bushes in rows in a triangular flowerbed. The first row has only one rose bush. If each row has two more rose bushes than the preceding row, how many rose bushes are in the 5th row?

response.feedback: The student got this item correct.
Good try, but incorrect. You wrote the total number of rose bushes. There are 9 rose bushes in the 5th row.
Student seems to have counted all the rose bushes, and not only the number in the 5th row.
Sorry, that was incorrect. Each row has 2 more bushes than the previous row, giving the 5th row 9 rose bushes.
Seems that each row in this solution has one more than the previous row.
Sorry, that was incorrect. Each row has 2 more bushes than the previous row, giving the 5th row 9 rose bushes.
The student did not get this item right.
